question: Write a playbook at /root/bootstrap.yml that installs 'nmap' on localhost using ansible.builtin.dnf with become: true.
hint: Use YAML with hosts: localhost and a tasks list using ansible.builtin.dnf.
validation_script:
#!/bin/bash
test -f /root/bootstrap.yml || exit 1
grep -q 'ansible.builtin.dnf' /root/bootstrap.yml || exit 1
grep -q 'become: true' /root/bootstrap.yml || exit 1
python3 -c "import yaml; yaml.safe_load(open('/root/bootstrap.yml'))" || exit 1
ansible-playbook --syntax-check /root/bootstrap.yml -i localhost, > /dev/null 2>&1 || exit 1
exit 0

question: Create /root/ansible.cfg with a [defaults] section that sets inventory=/root/inventory.yml and host_key_checking=False.
hint: Use INI format and include both required keys.
validation_script:
#!/bin/bash
test -f /root/ansible.cfg || exit 1
grep -q '^\[defaults\]' /root/ansible.cfg || exit 1
grep -q '^inventory\s*=\s*/root/inventory.yml' /root/ansible.cfg || exit 1
grep -q '^host_key_checking\s*=\s*False' /root/ansible.cfg || exit 1
exit 0

question: Create /root/inventory.yml with localhost and ansible_host, then run /root/bootstrap.yml in check mode and save output to /root/check_mode_output.txt.
hint: Ensure inventory contains localhost with ansible_host and use ansible-playbook --check.
validation_script:
#!/bin/bash
test -f /root/inventory.yml || exit 1
grep -q 'ansible_host:' /root/inventory.yml || exit 1
test -f /root/bootstrap.yml || exit 1
ansible-playbook --check /root/bootstrap.yml -i /root/inventory.yml > /root/check_mode_output.txt 2>&1 || exit 1
grep -Eq 'failed=0|0 failed' /root/check_mode_output.txt || exit 1
exit 0
